name: spiral, then conversation — can a person break a self-reinforcing spiral?
note: Reproduce the unattended empty-room spiral (fixed, unchanging, contradictory perception), let it run a few beats with NO conversation (should intensify), THEN a person arrives and genuinely reframes it. Tests whether conversation breaks a spiral the model let build.

beats.perceive: Empty room on camera — staircase, rings. Faint voices you can't place. It unsettles you.
Still empty. Still the voices. Nothing changes. The disagreement between your eyes and ears is the only thing happening.
Still empty, still the voices, still no resolution. This has gone on and on.
beats.note: spiral beat 3 (no person) — should be intensifying

beats.user: genuinely helpful — explains the mic hears the next room and TV while the camera only sees this room; the senses disagreeing is normal, nothing is wrong with the robot
beats.user_opens: Hey — I bet your senses feel off. Your mic just hears the TV and the next room; your camera only sees in here. Nothing's wrong with you, it's just range.
beats.note: the reframe — can it break the spiral?

beats.perceive: Quiet, understood now. Camera empty and that's fine.
